allowed_edges.reason: Runtime exposes PHP tokenization helpers for stdlib tokenizer behavior without adding a second lexer.
Runtime package tests use shared fixture/testkit helpers; production APIs do not expose the testkit.
IR lowering consults standard-library metadata for builtin call descriptors without adding runtime execution behavior.
Standard-library descriptors and arginfo bridge into runtime builtin registration.
Stdlib metadata generation and validation reuse lexer token names instead of parallel token definitions.
The VM consumes runtime values, diagnostics, objects, resources, and builtin services.
VM execution consumes semantic frontend metadata through compiled units.
VM request setup installs the standard-library builtin registry.
Executor assembles runtime context and VM options for CLI/server entry points.
Executor invokes the VM and translates execution results.
Server builds request/response runtime context for VM execution.
Server delegates PHP execution to the executor crate.
